message.content:
Adversarially verify this finding about a design doc (the doc is at /tmp/claude-1000/-home-jleechan-projects-worktree-runner/8ca589dd-8422-44b1-a543-52bd7b71f14b/scratchpad/gistfile1.txt; the repo is /home/jleechan/projects/worktree_runner; you may use WebSearch/WebFetch and read files). Try to REFUTE it. Finding from the "infra-fit" reviewer, severity major:
TITLE: No health monitoring, self-healing, or alerting anywhere in the gha-isolated design
DETAIL: The existing setup runs a dense monitoring mesh that the proposal entirely omits: (a) self-hosted-colima/scripts/monitor.sh — 15-min cron, flock-locked, checks VM state, container health, disk ≥75%, GitHub registration, queue depth ≥50 pending/queued, and ZOMBIE detection (container Up locally but deregistered on GitHub ≥5 min → force-recreate; this closes a real silent-failure mode of Runner.Listener). (b) self-hosted-oss/preflight.sh — memory_pressure ≥80%, disk free <5 GB on ~/.colima, fseventsd RSS >2048 MB → immediate colima restart (root cause of silent dockerd hangs, Colima issue #1569). (c) self-hosted-oss/heal-runners.sh — stale offline registration cleanup, flapping-container recycle on error patterns ('Error: Conflict|Runner not found|signature is not valid'), restart-count threshold 6, gh busy-check before recreate. (d) mac-runner-health.sh / ubuntu-runner-health.sh — 5-min monitors with debounced Slack+email alerts (fire on OK→FAIL transition, 1h sustained-fail cooldown, recovery message), and a CPU-aware load threshold (70% of nproc — commit e9084fb365 fixed false positives where a 32-core box tripped a flat load1=4.99 threshold at 15% utilization). (e) self-hosted-oss/slack-alert.sh — severity-coded (info/warning/critical) webhook posts with silent no-op when unconfigured.
RECOMMENDATION: Treat monitoring/healing/alerting as a first-class module in any replacement, not a 'future step'. Minimum carry-over: zombie-runner detection against the GitHub API, queue-depth alerting, disk thresholds, debounced Slack alerts with severity, and CPU-relative (not absolute) load thresholds.
Is the finding factually correct and does the recommendation make sense? If the factual basis is wrong or the severity is inflated, refute it. Default to refuted=true only if you have concrete contrary evidence or the claim is unsupported after checking.

input.reasoning:
I attempted to refute the finding and could not — every factual claim checks out against the repo, and the design doc genuinely contains zero monitoring content.

DESIGN DOC OMISSION (confirmed): grep of /tmp/claude-1000/.../scratchpad/gistfile1.txt for monitor|health|heal|alert|zombie|slack|watchdog|observ returns zero hits across all 292 lines. The doc's only nod to observability is a `status()` stub that prints "Status: Not yet implemented" (line 269), and the "Next Steps" list (lines 288-291) covers runner.rs, service install, config model, and prerequisite installation — monitoring is not even listed as future work.

FACTUAL SPOT-CHECKS (all confirmed in repo):
(a) /home/jleechan/projects/worktree_runner/self-hosted-colima/scripts/monitor.sh — "Runs every 15 minutes via the user crontab" (line 6), flock re-exec with -w 60 (lines 88-92), DISK_THRESHOLD_PCT=75 (line 53), PENDING_THRESHOLD=50 / QUEUED_THRESHOLD=50 (lines 54-55), ZOMBIE_GRACE_SEC=300 with force-recreate of runners "Up locally but not online on GitHub" (lines 64, 290-340). Exactly as described.
(b) self-hosted-oss/preflight.sh — MEMORY_PRESSURE_CRITICAL=80 (line 29), DISK_FREE_MIN_GB=5 (line 30), FS_EVENTSD_RSS_KILL_MB=2048 (line 31), short-circuit to colima restart (lines 10-11, 130, 146). Confirmed.
(c) self-hosted-oss/heal-runners.sh + defaults.sh — RUNNER_SELF_HEAL_RESTART_THRESHOLD=6 (defaults.sh:141), RUNNER_SELF_HEAL_ERROR_PATTERNS="Error: Conflict|Runner not found|signature is not valid" (defaults.sh:145, verbatim match to the finding), clean_stale_offline() (heal-runners.sh:100), gh busy check via GH_BUSY map (line 131). Confirmed.
(d) self-hosted-oss/ubuntu-runner-health.sh — debounced alerts firing on OK→FAIL transition with recovery message on FAIL→OK (header lines 14-17), HEALTH_CHECK_INTERVAL_SEC=3600 sustained-fail cooldown (line 56), LOAD_THRESHOLD_PCT_OF_NPROC=70 with the exact motivating example "32-core host tripping on load1=4.99 while only 15% utilized" (lines 28-34, 54); commit e9084fb365 exists ("fix(self-hosted-oss): make ubuntu-runner-health load threshold CPU-aware (#7773)"); both health plist templates specify 5-min StartInterval (launchd templates line 28). Confirmed.
(e) self-hosted-oss/slack-alert.sh — severity info/warning/critical with per-severity colors (lines 18-20, 52-54) and "SILENT NO-OP" when SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL unset (lines 11, 37-40). Confirmed.

SEVERITY NOT INFLATED: The strongest counterargument is that the proposal's ephemeral runners + Scale Sets structurally reduce some failure modes (persistent zombie registrations, flapping long-lived containers). But this only mitigates part of one item: host-level failure modes the current mesh guards against — disk fill, silent dockerd/VM hangs, queue backlog from dead capacity, and any alerting at all — are orthogonal to ephemerality, and the doc neither claims gha-outrunner provides monitoring nor mentions the topic. For an "infra-fit" review of a replacement for a production setup whose monitoring exists because of real incidents (fseventsd runaway, zombie Runner.Listener, false-positive load alerts), "major" is a fair severity and the recommended minimum carry-over list is sensible. One nuance: zombie-detection semantics would need adapting for ephemeral Scale Set runners (stuck listener/scale-set lag rather than deregistered persistent runners), but that refines the recommendation rather than refuting it.
